title: I Never Saw A Moor,
content:
I never saw a moor,
    I never saw the sea;
    Yet know I how the heather looks,
    And what a wave must be.

    I never spoke with God,
    Nor visited in heaven;
    Yet certain am I of the spot
    As if the chart were given.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "I Never Saw A Moor,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
